patches.find: Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well.
patches.replace: The dome's claimed rate is asserted to match observation, not derived from the aetheric vortex geometry — and as the post-hoc paragraph below documents, the claimed 0.0466 deg/yr rate cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset by any standard metric, so the assertion of a match dissolves on contact with data.
patches.rationale: WIN-006.c3 hole2: 'Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well' is directly contradicted two paragraphs later by the finding that the dome's 0.0466 deg/yr rate cannot be reproduced by any standard metric. Replacing with an accurate framing that anticipates the post-hoc finding.

patches.find: we could not locate a pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author
patches.replace: no pre-registration of the expected Polaris/Sigma-Octantis brightness ratio exists in any public venue accessible to us, and the data source (Gaia DR3 stellar parameters) had been in the public record before the dome's earliest dated commit claiming this WIN
patches.rationale: WIN-033.c3 hole4: 'could not locate' is weaker than warranted. The Gaia DR3 data was published (2022) and publicly available; the soft 'could not locate' framing invites a dome response of 'well, I pre-registered it somewhere you couldn't find.' Stronger: no such venue exists in accessible public record.
